description: Valid skill configuration for Claude Code

location.commands: .claude/commands/[name].md (legacy, still works; skills take precedence if same name)

description.description: What the skill does and when to use it. Claude uses this to decide when to apply the skill. If omitted, uses first paragraph of markdown content.

name.description: Skill identifier, used for /skill-name invocation. If omitted, uses directory name. Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only (max 64 chars).

disable-model-invocation.description: If true, skill is only invoked manually via /skill-name (removed from Claude's context entirely). If false (default), Claude may apply it automatically when relevant.

user-invocable.description: If false, hidden from the / menu. Use for background knowledge users shouldn't invoke directly. Only Claude can invoke it.

argument-hint.description: Hint shown during autocomplete to indicate expected arguments.

allowed-tools.description: Tools Claude can use without asking permission when this skill is active.

model.description: Model to use when this skill is active.

context.description: If set to 'fork', the skill runs in a forked sub-agent context rather than the main conversation. The skill content becomes the prompt driving the subagent.

agent.description: Which subagent type to use when context: fork is set. Options: built-in agents (Explore, Plan, general-purpose) or custom subagent name. Default: general-purpose.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks scoped to this skill (same format as global hooks)

variables.$ARGUMENTS: Replaced with all user-provided arguments after /skill-name. If not present in content, appended as 'ARGUMENTS: <value>'.
variables.$ARGUMENTS[N]: Access a specific argument by 0-based index (e.g. $ARGUMENTS[0] for the first argument).
variables.$N: Shorthand for $ARGUMENTS[N] (e.g. $0 for first argument, $1 for second).
variables.${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}: The current session ID. Useful for logging or session-specific files.
variables.${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}: The directory containing the skill's SKILL.md file. Use to reference bundled scripts/files.

dynamicContext.description: The !`command` syntax runs shell commands before the skill content is sent to Claude. Command output replaces the placeholder.
dynamicContext.note: This is preprocessing, not something Claude executes. Claude only sees the final result.

structure.description: YAML frontmatter followed by markdown content. Skills can include supporting files in their directory.
structure.example:
---
name: skill-name
description: What this skill does
disable-model-invocation: true
---

# Skill Content

Instructions here...

bundledSkills.description: Skills that ship with Claude Code and are available in every session

skills./batch: Orchestrate large-scale changes across a codebase in parallel using git worktrees
skills./claude-api: Load Claude API and Agent SDK reference material
skills./debug: Troubleshoot current Claude Code session by reading debug log
skills./loop: Run a prompt repeatedly on an interval
skills./simplify: Review recently changed files for code reuse, quality, and efficiency

notes.hotReloading: Skills modified in ~/.claude/skills or .claude/skills are immediately available without restarting the session.
notes.additionalDirs: Skills in .claude/skills/ within --add-dir directories are loaded automatically and support live change detection.
notes.nestedDiscovery: Claude Code discovers skills from nested .claude/skills/ directories (supports monorepo setups).
notes.contextBudget: Skill descriptions are loaded at 2% of context window (fallback: 16,000 chars). Override with SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET env var.
notes.permissionControl: Control skill access via permissions: Skill(name) for exact match, Skill(name *) for prefix match.
